How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-06 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags stocks appearing in 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day; multi-screener agreement filters noise and amplifies directional signal quality.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PANW is the clear standout at score 898 - triple confluence of CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, and VOLUME_LEADER - though its matched_combo field is null, meaning no validated combo fires. AMAT mirrors the same three screeners at score 400, making it the only direct peer to PANW in construction. The score distribution is sharply top-heavy: PANW dwarfs the field, AMAT sits alone in the mid-range, and the remaining eight names cluster tightly between 95 and 232 with no validated-combo boosts lifting any of them. NEUTRAL macro at 50.93 provides no sector or regime tailwind to lean on, and the absence of any matched_combo across all ten names means today's batch sits below the threshold where the backtest edge is cleanest. Take PANW first at moderate conviction for the score lead and screener alignment, but size conservatively until a validated combo triggers.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: TRV (score 5, target ~$325.41 (DD20d -4.3%)).

3 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day open-interest delta direction and EMA8/21 trend alignment to filter noise from genuine positioning.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $XRT $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XRT ↑64 Watch$87.51 -0.62%BULLISH SWEEPCALL87.5$374KOPENING188167STRONG33
2QQQ ↑50 Watch$721.93 +1.31%BULLISH SWEEPCALL721$1589KNOT_FOUND—79STRONG47
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape shows 5 resolved trades at 100% win rate, but the verdict is explicitly WATCH - the key number is 5 resolved versus the 20-trade minimum required, which makes it impossible to confirm or contradict the backtest's 68.1% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XRT leads the batch at a score of 64 on a bullish sweep - notable because it sits just below the 65 threshold where the screener has historically carried real conviction, and retail ETF flow tends to front-run sector rotation. QQQ prints 50, a Watch-tier floor reading with no differentiated signal beyond the sweep pattern itself. Two signals total, zero names at 65 or above, and the score distribution is as thin as it gets - median and max are separated by only 14 points across a two-name batch. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 50.93, offering no amplifying tailwind for either setup. If pressed, XRT first at the higher score, but this is a Watch-tier, reduced-size day, not a conviction entry.

🧠 Ranks stocks where 30-day implied volatility structurally exceeds realized volatility, capturing the variance risk premium with Sinclair sector adjustments and value filters.
Construction
IV30 vs HV30 spread + sector bias (Sinclair Table 4.7: Utilities +31%, Tech -8%) + value factors (P/E, RoE, mcap). Score 0-100 ranks premium-selling candidates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the screener: a 67% win rate and +3.2% average return across 470 resolved trades beats SPY by 4.3 points, and monthly cohorts show a sequential climb from April's 51% to June's 83%, supporting the 2.34 backtest Sharpe as live-validated rather than curve-fitted.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG (78) leads a utility-heavy batch, followed by NEE (77) and CL (76) as the only names worth separating from the pack. All 40 signals fall in a compressed 65-78 band, median 68, with nothing clearing 80 - the distribution offers no score breakout to anchor high conviction. The macro regime reads NEUTRAL at 50.93, which is acceptable for variance premium selling but provides no tailwind, and the utility-dominated picks fit Sinclair's structural finding that utilities carry a +31% implied premium bias. Pattern fields are null across the board, so differentiation is score-rank alone. PCG first at moderate conviction, sized conservatively given the absence of any name clearing 80.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

EBAY at 100 and USO at 99 headline the batch, both rated Very strong and sitting at the top of the VRP alignment range. GDS at 97 adds a third name worth attention in the lead tier. The distribution is top-heavy but broadly populated: 13 of 30 signals clear 80, median is 75, and 22 of 30 reach 65 or above, so the signal quality is genuine rather than thin. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.93 offers no directional tailwind and argues against pressing size across the board. Take EBAY first given the max score of 100 and medium conviction on USO as a secondary.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is the immediate problem: all 50 signals scored 100, median 100, making ranking impossible within the batch. Among the listed names, ALL (Allstate) and AMKR (Amkor Technology) carry the most institutional credibility for the hedging thesis given their size and liquidity, but every pick ties at score 100 with no pattern data to separate any of them. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.93 neither blocks the setup nor endorses it. This batch is ceiling-compressed and stripped of ranking signal. If pressed, ALL first at low conviction only - the absence of score dispersion across all 50 names suggests a trigger calibration issue today, not a clean opportunity set.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tracker has zero resolved signals (verdict: WATCH) so direct comparison to the 65% backtest win rate is premature, but the time-boxed proxy at 50.46% sits below backtest and the monthly cohorts, while trending up from February's 24.6% to June's 59.3%, have not yet closed the gap.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 430 closed · WR TB 50% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

LAC leads at 91, the only Very strong name today, though pattern data is null across the board so every thesis rests on the drawdown/RSI setup alone. Start with LAC at 1-2% basket sizing per the LEAPS construction, then add BABA second given its liquidity depth.

🧠 Identifies vol-of-vol climax conditions using VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to flag premium-selling setups at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑62 Watch$749.01 +0.57%SELL VOL1.43
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape returns a KEEP verdict at 62.5% win rate and +1.47% average that clears SPY by just 4 basis points, but the key number is June 2026, which went 0-for-2 at -1.68% average, a hard reversal from April's 100% win rate at +5.4%, indicating the edge has deteriorated sharply in the most recent resolved cohort.

🧠 Flags options sweeps exceeding 5x the 20-day volume mean with premium over $50K, filtered to calm non-extended tickers to isolate informed positioning.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Monthly cohorts from March through June all post 100% 20-day win rates, and the 90-day tape matches at 100% on 18 resolved trades - but 18 sits below the system's own 20-trade minimum for a valid verdict, so the 195% average return against SPY's -0.24% reflects options leverage in a rising tape more than confirmed screener edge against the backtest's 61% win rate.

CRDO leads at a score of 78 and Signal tier, but its BEARISH SWEEP pattern sits against a LONG direction tag - that conflict needs resolution before sizing. XLU is the standout at 95 (Very Strong), with XLC and XLE both printing 80 (Strong) - three names clear the top tier out of 11. The distribution is top-heavy: 3 signals at 80-plus, 6 at 65-plus, median exactly at 65, meaning the lower half of the batch is borderline and likely not actionable. Pattern data is null across every pick, so tier and score are carrying all the weight with no structural confirmation. A utilities ETF leading a momentum screen against a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 50.93) reads defensive rotation, not broad risk-on acceleration, which tempers the XLU signal. Take XLU first at reduced size given the macro headwind; pass on XLK at 55 entirely.

🧠 Finds stocks down 40%+ from 52w high showing capitulation volume and a reversal candle, targeting mean reversion from forced-selling exhaustion.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live 90d tracking at 58.2% WR and avg +5.4%, beating SPY by 6.6 pts, broadly confirms the backtest's 63.3% WR, and monthly cohorts recovering from a -12.9% avg in January to +3.3% in June reinforce the KEEP verdict - the key number is that +6.6 pt SPY spread holding across a volatile six-month window.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks by 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, passing only price-above-MA100 names with SPY above MA200, to isolate clean trend momentum.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when at least two intermarket correlations snap beyond 2-sigma in seven days, betting on mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Captures post-earnings continuation in stocks with a positive EPS surprise and a held gap, exploiting institutional under-reaction amplified by analyst upward revisions.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$11.52 +0.04%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports the thesis at 61% WR and avg +4.5% against SPY's -0.1%, a 4.6-point edge that justifies the KEEP verdict, but the June cohort's compression to 51% WR and +2.1% avg is a live signal that drift is fading in the current tape.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms the screener works at 71% win rate and +4.3% average beating SPY by 5.4 points (verdict: KEEP), but the monthly cohorts partially undercut that headline - May 2026 flipped to a -1.1% average with a 49% win rate before June recovered to 60% WR and +2.3%, and that May break is the key number showing the edge degrades sharply when the underlying tape turns adverse.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely ceiling-compressed: all 50 signals print at 100 with a median of 100, which eliminates internal ranking and is the first thing to flag about this batch. Among the top picks, AMZZ, ANGL, and ARCO each carry score 100 and Very strong tier, but pattern is null across every name, stripping out any secondary catalyst context. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (50.93), adding neither a confirming tailwind nor a structural headwind. The combination of undifferentiated scores, absent pattern data, and a flat macro regime makes this batch weak on selectivity. If forced to act, take ARCO first at low conviction and size it small until pattern data or a macro tilt provides a cleaner edge.

Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape with a 57% win rate and avg +4.0% beating SPY by +5.5 points confirms the KEEP verdict, and the monthly cohort trend from April's avg -0.73% to May's +4.04% to June's +4.46% adds directional support, but neither datapoint rescues the Very Strong tier, which is the exact tier dominating today's batch and which carries the worst realized numbers in the system.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Five names print at the max score of 95 - ACHC, ANAB, BEAM, BV, and DFH - all tagged PRIMED, with CCCC and CHH close behind at 90 and ABEO rounding out the notable names at 85 Strong. The score distribution is top-heavy: all 50 signals clear 65, 23 clear 80, and the top cluster is unusually dense at the ceiling. That density is a warning, not a gift - the 90-day tier breakdown shows Very Strong names post a 35% win rate and avg return of -7.6%, the worst cohort in the system by a wide margin, badly trailing Signal and Watch names which both run near 58% and positive avg. NEUTRAL macro at 50.93 neither adds fuel nor removes it, but the mismatch between today's crowded top scores and their historically poor forward returns argues for sizing down across the board. If forced to pick one name first, ACHC on the basis that behavioral health names can carry idiosyncratic catalysts, but conviction is low and the tier data says patience beats aggression here.

🧠 Identifies stocks with the highest relative volume surge across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum to flag volume-driven breakout leaders.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

The Very strong tier carries the worst live track record of any tier (WR 36%, avg -8.0%), so a perfect score today is not a signal upgrade. Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The recent tape confirms the KILL verdict and contradicts any edge thesis: the 90-day cohort posts a 37% win rate and avg -7.9% vs SPY -1.8% (a -6.1 pt gap), and the monthly trend reinforces deterioration, with June crashing to 32.5% WR and avg -11% after 52%-plus win rates and positive avg returns in both April and May.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects rejection-tail bars at value-area extremes using Market Profile, with edge from price reversing away from an excess tail when location is extreme.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.48 +0.64%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape (5 resolved, 80% win rate, avg +4.16% versus SPY at -0.39%) and the sequential monthly cohorts (June at 100% win rate with avg +4.64%, May at 67% with avg +3.85%) are both directionally consistent with the backtest's +3.18% filtered mean, but 5 resolved trades is the key number - it is one-quarter of the 20 minimum needed to move the verdict off WATCH, so the signal is promising but unconfirmed.

🧠 Catches PEAD continuation by flagging a weekly pullback-then-breakout within four weeks of a positive earnings gap, entering as price reclaims the prior week's high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

🧠 Isolates institutional flow signals by skipping entries where KVO crossovers or Force Index flips fire within three days, using volume noise as a negative gate.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AGL is the lone name above 65 today, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier, but that tier has produced the worst live returns of any tier in 90-day tracking, a 36.5% win rate and -4.9% average. Everything else in the batch is Watch-tier, with ABVX the next best at 59 and HYMC, CLYM, and SLS clustered between 34 and 36, and pattern data is null across all eight names. At a median score of 19 across 35 signals, this distribution is severely bottom-heavy with only one name clearing 65. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.93 provides no directional tailwind, and the SPY filter cannot manufacture edge that the setup itself is not generating. If forced to act, AGL is the only name worth attention, but size it small given the Very strong tier's live track record.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The batch is informationally flat: all 50 signals score between 99 and 100, meaning the model has lost ranking resolution and is outputting a binary pass rather than a gradient, which strips any intra-batch differentiation. AMD and PANW are the two names worth watching first given options liquidity and sector leadership in semis and cybersecurity respectively, though neither carries a pattern flag and their scores are indistinguishable from the six names behind them (DELL, DDOG, AMAT, UMC, ASX, STM). The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 50.93) neither supports nor penalizes the long bias, and the batch reads as undifferentiated tape momentum with no catalyst anchor. Conviction is low given the score compression and a REJECTED backtest; if forced to act, AMD is the most tradeable name on a day trade basis only, sized minimally until the model produces scores with actual dispersion.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking argues against the REJECT verdict, posting 53% WR and beating SPY by 2.9 points across 479 resolved trades, but the monthly cohort WR has eroded from 78.7% in March to 52.4% in June, and with all backtest stats null there is no historical floor to determine whether this decay is normal mean reversion or something structural.

🧠 Flags unusual volume, premium %, and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs, weighting flow intensity against spot crypto correlation to score momentum signals.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $HUT $BITO $FBTC

3FBTC ↑50 Watch$53.55 +0.05%44.68-0.2%neutral

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMIN tops the batch at 76 - an India small-cap ETF, which is an unconventional lead for a themes screen and warrants scrutiny before sizing. WCLD at 72 and CLOU at 67 both flag cloud computing, the only theme with two names in the top half, suggesting internal momentum there over the others. No pattern data is populated for any ticker, so there is no technical trigger to anchor an entry. The score distribution is compressed and low-conviction: max is 76, median is 61, and nothing clears 80, meaning the screen is producing soft signals rather than high-confidence calls. NEUTRAL macro at 50.93 adds no directional push, so if forced to act, WCLD first at minimal size with the expectation this batch needs a stronger regime read before committing capital.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE leads at 100 but carries no pattern detail, making it a score-topper with thin qualitative support. JEF at 98 is the most legible name in the batch - insider buying at an investment bank is notable given the sector's sensitivity to deal flow and rate sentiment, though the null pattern field strips out any technical read. SMMT and COE both sit at 98 with the same data gaps. The score distribution is nearly useless for discrimination: all 50 signals score above 80 and the median is 94, which means the ranking collapses into a single compressed band rather than a real gradient. A NEUTRAL macro at 50.93 provides no directional overlay to sharpen conviction. Take JEF first on name recognition and sector logic, but keep size small given the absent pattern confirmation across the entire batch.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers land at a perfect score of 100 in the Very strong tier - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM - but all pattern fields are null, so the data gives no basis to differentiate them. The score distribution is top-heavy: median 70 across 50 signals, 12 at 80 or above, 41 clearing 65, meaning the batch clusters toward the ceiling rather than spreads broadly. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 50.93 neither amplifies nor cuts against the signal. The key caution is that the Very strong tier historically posts the weakest avg return of the three tiers at 3.43%, well below Watch at 9.54%, so the flush of perfect scores looks impressive but tier-level data argues for reduced size - take BL first among the eight at low-to-moderate conviction pending a manual setup review.

Construction

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML leads at 97 (Very strong) and FB at 93 (Very strong), both reflecting heavy new and increased activity across the whale universe. AVGO checks in at 79, just below the high-conviction threshold, with HUT, TSM, MU, and V bunched between 74 and 77. Only 2 of 50 signals clear 80 and the median sits at 55, so the batch is thin at the top and noisy below it. Macro is NEUTRAL at 50.93, providing no directional cover for the long-only tilt, and every signal here is 60-90 days stale by filing-lag design. ASML is the name to put on the desk first given the score gap, but this is a watch-list update rather than a fresh entry trigger, medium conviction only.

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging within LATE_CHASE and RIDING sectors, scoring each by sector relative strength multiplied by stock underperformance to flag mean-reversion catch-up setups.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Today's batch is undifferentiated and uninvestable at current read: all 26 signals scored exactly 50, the maximum in the dataset, with zero names clearing 65 and zero clearing 80, meaning the screener found no genuine separation. CEG and CWEN are the most recognizable names given their positioning in the power and clean-energy space, but their scores are identical to every other ticker, and no pattern data exists on any pick. Pass on all names today and wait for a refresh that produces at least one signal above 65.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks the investable universe cheapest-first on EV/EBIT, targeting mean reversion in statistically depressed enterprise valuations, excluding financials and utilities.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape has no resolved trades - 0 of 53 - and while a partial tracking subset of 18 shows a 55.6% win rate, that sample is too thin and the backtest too absent to confirm or contradict any edge.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
HALF SIZEinsufficient data (0 resolved, need ≥20)
Last 90 days: 53 signals captured
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 18 closed · WR TB 56% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ scores a perfect 100, WRD hits 97, and KROS lands at 93, all in the Very Strong tier, making them the clearest reads today. KROS is the notable outlier - a biotech appearing near the top of an EV/EBIT deep value screen points to either unusually positive operating earnings or aggressive market markdown, and deserves a hard fundamental check before any position. The score distribution is top-heavy and solid, with 7 of 16 signals at or above 80 and a median of 73, so this is not a thin or scattered batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 50.93) offers no tailwind for deep value names, keeping the setup honest but not hostile. BZ is the first-look name given its top score, at low-to-moderate conviction only, since no backtest exists and no chart pattern data is available for any name in the batch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.

🧠 Flags retail-driven euphoria via call-volume spikes, Reddit mention surges, and parabolic price action absent an earnings catalyst; fades the momentum short.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, targeting long vol setups before realized volatility expansion.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $ASML
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑65● Signal$337.90 +1.03%PRE_IV_RAMP11—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2ASML ↑55 Watch$1829.05 +3.38%PRE_IV_RAMP12—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline, flagging elevated hedging demand as a contrarian signal when the underlying is quiet.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $COST $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1COST ↓91●●● Very strong$952.28 +0.06%77%75—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
2MSTR ↑55 Watch$96.18 -4.55%66%1000.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape at 53.6% win rate and +8.8% average beats SPY by 7.6 points and supports KEEP, but the June cohort's drop to 37.5% win rate and -2.0% average is the key number, a clear recent deterioration the headline figure obscures.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Captures tickers where put IV overwhelms call IV at above the 90th skew percentile, flagging extreme put demand that marks unusual bearish options positioning.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$382.08 +6.00%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 There is no completed backtest to compare against (NOT_YET_BACKTESTED, only 3 qualifying battery trades as of 2026-06-09), so the 9-trade live sample is the sole evidence, and it fractures sharply by tier: 'Very strong' (n=4) shows 75% win rate at a plus-7.99% average while 'Strong' (n=2) shows 0% win rate at minus-55.92%, and June 2026's two fresh trades both lost averaging minus-24.5%, making the most recent cohort the loudest warning in the dataset.

🧠 Flags tickers where 30-day implied vol spikes above 20-day realized vol by more than 1.5 standard deviations, surfacing candidates to sell fear premium.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $COST
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1COST ↓100●●● Very strong$952.28 +0.06%77%23%3.35EXTREME_FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects false breakouts beyond the value area that are reclaimed the next session, mechanically trapping directional traders who chased the move.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1AVGO ↑60 Watch$382.08 +6.00%BEAR_TRAP366.64382.4218.16
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The backtest is empty (N=0, NOT_YET_BACKTESTED), so the live tape is the only statistical grounding available: 75% win rate and +5.8% average on 36 resolved trades over 90 days beats a negative SPY baseline by 6.9 points, and monthly cohorts running from May (76.9% / +8.0%) to June (73.9% / +4.6%) show mild softening in both metrics but no breakdown that would challenge the KEEP verdict earned purely from live performance.

🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and Kerberos-style flow scoring.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓85●● Strong$21.64 -1.79%54%861%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape contradicts the premise entirely: 26 resolved trades show a 27% win rate and -9.2% average 20-day return versus SPY at -0.5%, a -8.7-point gap, and the May 2026 cohort (n=24, the only statistically meaningful sample) posted an identical -9.1% average at 25% WR, confirming the KILL verdict is the key number, not the backtest that does not yet exist.

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens for financial distress via the Altman Z-score composite, flagging firms near insolvency thresholds where price lags fundamental deterioration.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a washout. One signal fired and scored 0, clearing no threshold - n_ge_65 and n_ge_80 are both zero, and top_picks_today is empty. The score distribution is flat at the floor, not clustered or top-heavy, just absent. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 50.93, which is a workable backdrop, but there is nothing here to work with. Pass today entirely.

🧠 STFR flags an undisclosed pattern via proprietary logic; construction detail is not surfaced in the available metadata.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA identifies institutional accumulation and distribution by comparing price spread and close position against volume, flagging divergences where effort and result conflict.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
